title: NATS Key-Value Store
description: Schema for NATS JetStream Key-Value Store bucket configuration and entry objects. The KV Store is a JetStream-backed abstraction providing immediately consistent associative array semantics. Buckets are implemented as JetStream streams with the KV_ name prefix. Each key-value entry is stored as a JetStream message where the NATS subject encodes the key and the payload is the value.

KeyValueBucketConfig.title: Key-Value Bucket Configuration
KeyValueBucketConfig.description: Configuration for creating a NATS KV bucket. Maps to a JetStream stream with KV-specific defaults and the KV_ name prefix.

bucket.description: Name of the KV bucket. Must consist of alphanumeric characters, underscores, or hyphens. The backing JetStream stream is created with the name KV_{bucket}.

description.description: Human-readable description of the bucket's purpose.

ttl.description: Time-to-live for entries in nanoseconds. Entries older than this duration are automatically purged. 0 means entries never expire.

storage.description: Backend storage type for bucket data.

num_replicas.description: Number of replicas for high availability in a JetStream cluster. Must not exceed the number of JetStream-enabled servers.

history.description: Number of historical values to keep per key. A history of 1 means only the latest value is retained. Values up to 64 are supported.

max_value_size.description: Maximum size in bytes for an individual value. -1 means no limit.

max_bytes.description: Maximum total bytes for the entire bucket across all keys and history. -1 means no limit.

sources.description: List of source streams to aggregate into this bucket. Enables cross-account or cross-cluster KV mirroring.

KeyValueEntry.title: Key-Value Entry
KeyValueEntry.description: A single entry in a NATS KV bucket, as returned by get and watch operations. Includes the key, value, metadata, and operation type.

bucket.description: Name of the KV bucket this entry belongs to.

key.description: The key identifying this entry within the bucket. Keys support dot-separated hierarchical namespaces, e.g. 'config.database.host'.

value.description: The stored value for this key, base64-encoded. May be arbitrary bytes.

revision.description: Monotonically increasing revision number for this key. Incremented on every put, delete, or purge operation for the key.

delta.description: Number of operations since this revision. Available in watch results to indicate how many updates were missed.

created.description: Timestamp when this revision was created.

operation.description: The operation that produced this entry. 'PUT' for new or updated values, 'DEL' for deleted keys, 'PURGE' for purged key history.

KeyValueBucketStatus.title: Key-Value Bucket Status
KeyValueBucketStatus.description: Runtime status for a KV bucket including current configuration, entry counts, and backing stream details.

values.description: Current number of unique keys in the bucket.

ttl.description: Configured entry TTL in nanoseconds. 0 means no expiry.

bytes.description: Total bytes currently stored in the bucket.

is_mirror.description: Whether this bucket mirrors another bucket.

RePublish.title: Re-Publish Configuration
RePublish.description: Configuration for republishing messages to a different subject when they are stored in the stream backing this KV bucket.

src.description: Source subject filter for messages to republish. Supports wildcards.

dest.description: Destination subject to republish matching messages to.

headers_only.description: If true, only republish headers without the message body. Reduces bandwidth for consumers that only need metadata.

StreamSource.title: Stream Source
StreamSource.description: Reference to a source stream for mirroring or aggregating data into this KV bucket's backing stream.

name.description: Name of the source stream to mirror from.

opt_start_seq.description: Sequence number to start mirroring from.

filter_subject.description: Subject filter to apply when sourcing from the named stream.

external.description: Configuration for sourcing from a stream in another account or cluster.

api.description: JetStream API prefix in the remote account, e.g. '$JS.hub.API'.

deliver.description: Subject prefix for delivering messages from the remote account.
